title: pavonia mutisii
intro: Pavonia mutisii, also known as rose mallow or mountain hollyhock, is a flowering plant species in the mallow family. It is native to the Andes mountain range in South America and is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The plant has various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues, and is often used to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Pavonia mutisii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are consumed orally or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
